question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the native SQL query in findNetworkNodes() uses a LEFT JOIN to latest_node_stake, so nodes without stake data will have null stake fields (nullif(ns.max_stake, -1) returns null), causing mirror node consumers to receive node records with missing stake information that may be interpreted as incorrect data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LEFT JOIN to node_info in findNetworkNodes() so that nodes without a corresponding entry in the node table have null admin_key, grpc_proxy_endpoint, and associated_registered_nodes fields, causing mirror node consumers to receive incomplete node records that appear to have missing data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) as nodeAccountId' logic in findNetworkNodes() so that if the node table has a different account_id than the address_book_entry, the node table value takes precedence, causing mirror node consumers to receive an incorrect nodeAccountId in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeCertHash encoding logic ('0x' || convert_from(abe.node_cert_hash, 'UTF8')) in findNetworkNodes() where if node_cert_hash contains non-UTF8 bytes, convert_from() throws a PostgreSQL error, causing the entire node list query to fail and all node records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the serviceEndpointsJson subquery in findNetworkNodes() where if address_book_service_endpoint has no rows for a given node, the coalesce returns '[]'::jsonb, causing mirror node consumers to receive an empty service endpoints array instead of the correct endpoints, leading to incorrect network topology records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a limit of 1 (minimum valid value per @Min(1)) so that getEffectiveLimit() returns 1, and findNetworkNodes() returns only one node record, causing mirror node consumers to receive an artificially truncated view of the network that omits all other nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a limit of 1 so that PageRequest.of(0, 1, sort) is used, and if there are multiple registered nodes, only one is returned, causing mirror node consumers to receive an incomplete set of registered node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getRegisteredNodes() always uses PageRequest.of(0, ...) (page 0 only) with no cursor-based pagination, so if there are more registered nodes than the limit, mirror node consumers can never retrieve the full set of registered nodes through a single API call, causing systematically missing records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RangeOperator.GT parameter with value Long.MAX_VALUE-1 so that getInclusiveValue() returns Long.MAX_VALUE as lowerBound, and then a RangeOperator.LT parameter with value 1 so that getInclusiveValue() returns 0 as upperBound, causing lowerBound > upperBound to throw IllegalArgumentException and preventing any registered node records from being exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RangeOperator.GT parameter with value Long.MAX_VALUE so that getInclusiveValue() computes value+1 causing a long overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, making lowerBound a large negative number that passes the lowerBound > upperBound check, causing findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs() to return incorrect or unexpected registered node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RangeOperator.LT parameter with value 0 so that getInclusiveValue() computes value-1 = -1, but NumberRangeParameter.getNumberValue() rejects negative values with IllegalArgumentException before this point, meaning the attacker cannot directly trigger underflow but can cause an error that prevents any registered node records from being exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RangeOperator.GT nodeId parameter with value Long.MAX_VALUE-1 so that getInclusiveValue() returns Long.MAX_VALUE as lowerBound, and since upperBound defaults to Long.MAX_VALUE, lowerBound == upperBound passes the check, causing findNetworkNodes() to query for node_id >= Long.MAX_VALUE AND node_id <= Long.MAX_VALUE, returning an empty list and making all node records appear missing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RangeOperator.LT nodeId parameter with value 1 so that getInclusiveValue() returns 0 as upperBound, and since lowerBound defaults to 0, the range [0,0] is valid, causing findNetworkNodes() to query for node_id >= 0 AND node_id <= 0, returning only node with id=0 (if it exists) and making all other node records appear missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a timestamp Bound where getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns a value and adjustUpperBound() returns Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getFirstDayOfMonth(Long.MAX_VALUE, -1) to be called with a timestamp that overflows Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, Long.MAX_VALUE), producing an ArithmeticException that propagates as a 500 error and makes supply records missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a timestamp where maxTimestamp is in a month boundary (e.g., first nanosecond of a month) so that getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1) returns the first nanosecond of the previous month, and Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound) replaces a valid minTimestamp with this earlier value, causing getSupplyHistory() to search a wider window and potentially return a supply record from a different time period than requested?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the latest_address_book CTE selects only the most recent address book by start_consensus_timestamp DESC LIMIT 1, so if a new address book was recently submitted but not yet finalized, the query returns the previous address book's nodes, causing mirror node consumers to receive stale and incorrect node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_node_stake CTE which uses 'select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake' as a correlated subquery, so if node_stake is updated between the time the CTE is materialized and the main query runs, the stake data joined to node records may be from a different consensus point than the address book, causing incorrect stake values in exported node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.max_stake, -1) sentinel value logic in findNetworkNodes(), where if a node's max_stake is legitimately -1 (indicating no maximum), it is converted to null in the exported record, causing mirror node consumers to incorrectly interpret the node as having no max_stake data rather than an unlimited stake?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.min_stake, -1) sentinel value logic in findNetworkNodes(), where if a node's min_stake is legitimately -1, it is converted to null, causing mirror node consumers to receive incorrect min_stake data in exported node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.stake_not_rewarded, -1) sentinel value logic in findNetworkNodes(), where if stake_not_rewarded is legitimately -1, it is converted to null, causing mirror node consumers to receive incorrect stake_not_rewarded data in exported node records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the grpcProxyEndpointJson CASE expression in findNetworkNodes() where if n.grpc_proxy_endpoint is non-null but missing the 'port' key, (n.grpc_proxy_endpoint->>'port')::integer throws a PostgreSQL cast error, causing the entire node list query to fail and all node records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicKey CASE expression in findNetworkNodes() where if abe.public_key starts with '0x' it is returned as-is, but if it contains non-printable characters or is malformed, the exported publicKey field contains incorrect data that mirror node consumers cannot parse correctly?
